commit.message:
Add PKCE support for public clients (RFC 7636) (#127)

* Add PKCE support for public clients (RFC 7636) (#113)

Implement Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE) to allow public clients
(SPAs, mobile apps, CLI tools) to use the Authorization Code grant
securely without a client secret. Only S256 challenge method is
supported; plain is rejected as insecure.

- Add `public` flag to Client model and configuration
- Add `code_challenge` / `code_challenge_method` columns (V4 migration)
- Store PKCE params during authorization, verify at token exchange
- Public clients resolve via client_id only (no secret required)
- Expose `code_challenge_methods_supported` in OpenID Discovery
- Unit tests for PkceManager, parseCodeChallenge, ClientAuthenticationUtil

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add unit tests for TokenController grant-type routing and methods

Test getTokens routing (resolveClient vs resolveClientForAuthorizationCodeGrant),
getTokensUsingAuthorizationCode (missing code, redirect_uri mismatch, PKCE failure,
success), getTokensUsingRefreshToken (missing token, refreshed/fallback), and
getTokensUsingClientCredentials (access token only).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message: fix: eliminate dashboard flicker with @st.fragment and PNG chart rendering Root cause: Streamlit reruns the ENTIRE script on every widget interaction. After processing adds data, any click/hover/selectbox change recreates all matplotlib charts from scratch — visible as right-side flicker. Two-pronged fix: 1. @st.fragment on analytics_dashboard(): - Isolates the dashboard so it only reruns when its OWN widgets change - File uploader, Run button, sidebar slider no longer trigger dashboard rerun - Moved search/filter INTO the fragment (duplicated from sidebar) so they trigger fragment-only reruns, not full-page reruns 2. Charts rendered to PNG bytes via st.image() instead of st.pyplot(): - st.pyplot() creates/destroys a JS canvas on every rerun = flicker - st.image() just swaps an src = no flicker - _fig_to_png() helper renders matplotlib fig to bytes and closes it Also: bumped streamlit>=1.37.0 (required for @st.fragment), reduced CSS overrides to only essential layout stabilizers, set matplotlib backend to Agg. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6

commit.message:
fix: address code review findings round 2 — approval flow, routing, cost enforcement

Phase 1 — Approval flow end-to-end (P0):
- Fix Slack action ID mismatch: match robodev_approval_ prefix instead of
  robodev_approve_/robodev_reject_ which never matched the generated IDs
- Add ApprovalHandler interface to webhook server, route approval callbacks
  to controller instead of forwarding as spurious tickets
- Add ResolveApproval to controller: pre-start approval launches the job,
  pre-merge approval completes the task, rejection transitions to Failed
- Call RequestApproval at both pre-start and pre-merge gates
- Allow NeedsHuman → Failed state transition for rejection
- Watchdog now iterates NeedsHuman runs and cancels pending approvals on
  termination
- Wire approvalAdapter in main.go to bridge webhooks to controller

Phase 2 — Routing, diff, labels, docs (P1):
- Auto-derive webhook trigger labels from ticketing config when not
  explicitly set, preventing unfiltered issue processing
- Wire IntelligentSelector as primary engine selector with default
  selector as fallback via new SetFallback method
- Add GetDiff to scm.Backend interface; implement for GitHub (compare API
  with diff media type) and GitLab (compare API with unified diff)
- Review gate now fetches real diff instead of passing empty string
- URL-encode label name in GitHub removeLabel to handle spaces/specials
- Remove unused RequireHumanApprovalBeforeMR config field
- Fix docs: runAsUser 1000→10000, six not seven layers, PRM no longer
  coming soon, engine hooks Claude Code only, memory store note

Phase 3 — Runtime cost enforcement (P1):
- Add MaxCostPerJob to watchdog RulesConfig with checkTotalCost rule
- Wire guardrails.max_cost_per_job into watchdog rules in main.go

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Refactor: remove alloc feature gate, simplify to no_std+alloc (#7)

* Refactor: remove alloc feature gate, simplify to no_std+alloc

The three-tier split (no_std / alloc / std) was unnecessary — the library
requires alloc everywhere and core::error::Error is stable since Rust 1.81.

Remove the `alloc` feature and 50+ individual #[cfg(feature = "alloc")] gates.
Everything is now unconditional except codegen (needs proc-macro deps) and
the CLI (needs std). `--no-default-features` gives the full library in
no_std+alloc mode.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(vision): implement Vision AI Phase 0 -- shared service, model routing, room check register

Schoolgle Vision AI turns a phone camera into an intelligent compliance sensor.
A single room spin simultaneously updates estates compliance, asset register,
COSHH, helpdesk, safeguarding, and teaching & learning via Ed the chatbot.

Core service layer:
- lib/vision/types.ts -- VisionRequest, VisionResult, ModuleDispatch types
- lib/vision/models.ts -- Gemini Flash/Pro direct API + OpenRouter fallback routing
- lib/vision/service.ts -- analyzeVision() single entry point with model fallback
- lib/vision/dispatcher.ts -- routes findings to 7 modules with severity thresholds
- lib/vision/evidence.ts -- SHA-256 tamper-proof hashing, GPS, device fingerprint
- lib/vision/contexts/room-assessment.ts -- universal room scan prompt + parser

API endpoint:
- POST /api/vision/analyze -- accepts image + context, returns structured findings

Database migration (20260307_vision_ai_room_checks.sql):
- vision_scan_sessions -- every AI analysis performed
- room_check_schedule -- AM/PM check requirements per room
- room_checks -- tamper-proof evidence with auto-lock after 24h
- room_check_daily_status view -- dashboard green/red/amber
- coshh_register -- chemical inventory from vision scans
- product_consumption + school_budget_periods -- budget intelligence

Technical brief (docs/VISION_AI_TECHNICAL_BRIEF.md):
- Full architecture: Ed-first, per-pupil pricing, insurance positioning
- Cost model: ~$14-50/month AI cost per school, 81-86% gross margin
- 5-phase build plan with effort estimates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Shepherd v2: Full team orchestration with verified pipeline

Architecture:
- Role templates (architect, developer, qa, tracker) as full Claude Code projects
- Each role has its own CLAUDE.md, settings.json, and permission scope
- projects/ workspace pattern: templates copied per engagement, isolated state
- Pipeline rule: task files, runner scripts, state.json, retry protocol
- Tracker verifies every deliverable before pipeline advances
- Sequential execution, max 3 attempts per stage, escalation on failure
- Resume support via state.json — survives compaction and session restarts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix cache logic: throttle retries separately from display data

Previously: no valid cache + rate limit = hammers API every 20s, shows nothing
Now:
- Load valid cache for display regardless of age (stale data > no data)
- Separate attempt_stamp throttles fetches to once per TTL (60s default)
- Failed fetches don't reset the throttle, preventing rate limit storms
- Bump default TTL from 20s back to 60s

commit.message:
perf: Replace N+1 queries with bulk OR query in /api/produccion

Replaced per-pedido registros queries (batches of 5, 200ms delays)
with a single OR query that fetches all registros at once — same
pattern already used by tracking.js. Chunks into batches of 100
for Notion's compound filter limit. Reduces latency from ~12s to
~600ms for 100 pedidos, eliminating timeout risk on Vercel.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Balance: soften surges, increase late-game enemy HP scaling +10%

Enemy HP per-level bonus increased 0.14→0.154 for harder late-game scaling.

file exports. All 8 report types render inline: transactions table,
category/merchant bar charts, month-over-month pivot, income vs expenses
stat cards, recurring charges, tax summary with expand/collapse, and
account summary. Export (CSV/PDF/QBO) still works unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

research(perception): CW-94 Gemini Robotics-ER 1.5 analysis for CW-1

Comprehensive analysis of Google's Gemini Robotics-ER 1.5 VLM for
CW-1 perception stack integration. Covers: head-to-head comparison
with current YOLO26n + cuVSLAM + SegFormer stack, cost analysis,
on-device viability (none), VLM comparison (RT-2, OpenVLA, Octo),
IP risks, and "slow brain / fast brain" architecture recommendation.

Key finding: Gemini ER is valuable as a complementary cloud layer
for task planning and scene reasoning, but cannot replace the
on-device perception pipeline.

commit.message:
fix: hide auth overlay by default to prevent flash on authenticated load

Overlay was display:flex in CSS, making it visible on every page load.
Authenticated users saw it flash for the duration of the /auth/me round-trip.
Changed default to display:none — showOverlay() explicitly sets display:flex
when needed, so unauthenticated flow is unchanged.

commit.message:
fix: use relative URLs for HA ingress compatibility

HA Supervisor strips the ingress path prefix before forwarding to the
add-on, so all routes register at root. Browser-side JS now uses
relative URLs (e.g. 'api/status' instead of '/api/status') which
resolve correctly both locally and through HA ingress.
